text:
High Priestess Mareen withdrew with her attendants. Below, the priestesses gathered up the bodies of the sacrificed sailors to carry them to another part of the temple, where they would be readied for the feast. They had taken the souls. They would not waste the bodies.

Melusine walked in silence in the procession, telling herself again and again why it was necessary, as if that might calm her troubled conscience and her stomach, which lurched at the thought of the sacrificial feast. And yet the least she could do was honor those who had given their lives today.
